steps.title: Identify the narrative opening signal
steps.content: “Begin a narrative about the creation” is the key phrase. This signals that we need an opening—a sentence that establishes the foundation of the story, not one that describes mechanics or summarizes timeline.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
The correct answer opens with the inspiration source (ants forming bridges) and explicitly connects it to Demir's goal (“set out to design quadruped robots capable of similarly collaborative behavior”).

The other choices either describe how the robots operate (A), report how long the creation took (B), or focus on ant behavior rather than the human designer (D).

Opening a narrative about creation means starting with inspiration and intent—not mechanics or timeline.

C is correct.
